events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario involves EMT bending & installation on an industrial site. The observed field condition is "missing support within 3 ft of box." Work is in-progress.

NEC Article 358 covers EMT. Section 358.30(A) requires supports at least every 10 feet and within 3 feet of each outlet box, junction box, device box, etc. So missing support within 3 ft of a box is a non-compliance.

The work is in-progress, so this is a defect that needs correction. The decision should be "fail" since it's non-compliant. The component condition is "installed-defective" and "non-compliant."

Let me construct the JSON.

  "workable": [],

    "EMT run is missing required support within 3 ft of box, violating NEC 358.30(A)",
    "Unsupported section near box termination creates risk of conduit movement and box/connector strain"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop continuation of the run until a support is installed within 3 ft of the box",
    "Install an approved support/strap within 3 ft of the box per NEC 358.30(A)",
    "Verify all other box terminations on the run have compliant support spacing before proceeding",
    "Re-inspect the corrected support before continuing the EMT installation"
  ],
  "rationale": "NEC Art. 358.30(A) requires EMT to be securely supported within 3 ft of each box, cabinet, or fitting. The observed missing support near the box is a sequence/quality defect that must be corrected before the run can be extended. Since work is in-progress and the defect is present, the work fails the sequence check until the support is installed and verified.",
  "references": ["NEC Art. 358.30(A)", "NEC Art. 358", "NEC Ch. 9 (fill)"]
}
